steps.title: Identify the contrast in apparent distance
steps.content:
The key signal lies in each speaker's treatment of the moon's position.

In Text 1, “What seemed so far away Is but a child's balloon” shows that the moon, which ordinarily appears distant, is imagined as remarkably close; the line "Tangled in the tall mast's corded height," places it among the mast's cords.

In Text 2, the emphasis is reversed: “up so high?” and “Will you come down soon?” repeatedly underscore the moon's distance and unreachability.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
The correct response captures the essential contrast: Text 1 presents the moon as seeming very close (the sudden discovery of proximity in the mast), while Text 2 emphasizes distance (the moon is “up so high?” and unreachable).

The other choices mischaracterize the poems:
  • A mistakenly sees entrapment in both texts
  • B ignores Text 1's dismissive tone
  • C confuses Text 2's affectionate address for serious study


That confirms D is correct.
